reasoning_model_1: The model accurately generates the text '这么早就要起床，真是太难了。' with clear articulation. Regarding the core requirement 'Non-verbal vocalization' (yawning manner), the model appends a very distinct and realistic yawning sound effect at the end of the sentence (approx. 2.9s - 4.5s). However, the speech segment itself (0.0s - 2.8s) is delivered with a relatively standard projection and energy level, lacking the lethargy or prosodic distortion one would expect when speaking 'with a yawn'. The non-verbal feature is present but feels like a post-processing addition rather than an integrated manner of speaking.
reasoning_model_2: The model correctly produces the text content. It begins the audio with a non-verbal yawning/sighing sound (0.0s - 1.2s) which naturally transitions into the speech. Crucially, the speech delivery (1.3s - 4.0s) adopts a 'sleepy' persona—lower pitch, slower tempo, and a dragging tone—which perfectly complements the initial yawn. This holistic approach makes the non-verbal vocalization feel like an organic part of the speaker's state, rather than just an isolated sound effect. The performance effectively conveys the difficulty of waking up.
model_comparison: Both models successfully incorporate the requested 'Non-verbal vocalization' (yawn) into the audio. Model 1 (T1) places a high-quality yawn sound at the very end, but the preceding speech sounds too alert and disconnected from the act of yawning, resulting in a 'text + sound effect' feel. Model 2 (T2) places the yawn at the beginning and maintains a groggy, lethargic tone throughout the spoken text. T2 is superior because it integrates the non-verbal feature with the appropriate paralinguistic style (prosody) of the speech, fulfilling the command to 'say with a yawning manner' much more naturally.
